Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine, indicating significant renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance and color, supporting renal dysfunction and possible tubular damage. No evidence of urinary infection. Low serum globulin may suggest protein loss or decreased synthesis. Immediate nephrology evaluation is recommended.
